title: Stelis candida
taxonomy: Stelis candida belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification makes sense given the plant's unique and complex structure, which is characteristic of orchids. Within the Orchidaceae family, Stelis candida is further classified as a member of the subtribe Pleurothallidinae. The subtribe Pleurothallidinae is a group of orchids that are mostly found in Central and South America, which is consistent with the plant's native habitat. This classification helps to group Stelis candida with other similar species and provides insights into its evolutionary history.

intro: Stelis candida, also known as White Stelis, is a plant species that belongs to the orchid family (Orchidaceae) and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Stelis candida include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Stelis candida is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make tinctures and extracts that are used to treat various health conditions.
